prompt_text: LeadIQ vs Apollo.io pricing and features
response:
**Apollo.io** is generally the better choice if you want an **all-in-one outbound platform** with a large contact database, sequencing, dialer, and automation; **LeadIQ** is better if your team mainly needs **fast lead capture, verification, CRM sync, and job-change tracking** rather than a full outreach suite.[1][3][6]

| Category | **Apollo.io** | **LeadIQ** |
|---|---|---|
| **Best for** | All-in-one prospecting + outreach | Fast capture from LinkedIn/browser into CRM |
| **Core strength** | Large database and engagement tools | One-click capture, verification, workflow speed |
| **Database** | 275M+ contacts / 60M+ companies reported in sources[2][1] | Focuses on capture and verification; sources also mention a large database but position it more as a workflow tool[3][4] |
| **Sequencing** | Included[1][6] | Typically used alongside another sequencing tool[1][3] |
| **Dialer** | Included on higher plans[1][4] | Not positioned as a core feature in the cited sources[1][3] |
| **CRM sync** | Yes[6][2] | Strong emphasis on one-click CRM sync and field mapping[3] |
| **Job-change tracking** | Yes[3] | Yes, highlighted as a key feature[3] |
| **AI email help** | Yes, including AI-powered prospecting/writing in cited comparisons[3] | Yes, patented AI-powered personalized email generator is listed in LeadIQ materials[3] |

**Pricing is plan-dependent and the exact numbers vary a bit across sources**, but the overall pattern is consistent: Apollo’s entry plans are usually cheaper, while LeadIQ’s higher tiers can become more expensive per team as seats and usage grow.[1][2][4][6]

- **Apollo.io**: cited pricing includes a free plan, then roughly **$49–$59/user/month** for entry paid tiers, **$79–$99/user/month** for mid tiers, and higher organization/enterprise pricing above that.[2][4][6]
- **LeadIQ**: cited pricing includes a free plan, an **Essential** plan around **$39/user/month**, a **Pro** tier around **$79–$200/month** depending on billing/team context in the sources, and custom enterprise pricing.[1][2][4]

A practical way to choose between them:

- Choose **Apollo.io** if you want **database + sequencing + dialer + outreach automation** in one tool.[1][6]
- Choose **LeadIQ** if you already have sequencing/outreach tools and mainly need **faster prospect capture, cleaner data entry, and CRM enrichment**.[1][3]
- If your team is cost-sensitive, Apollo is often the **cheaper all-in-one option** for SMBs, while LeadIQ is more compelling when workflow speed is the main pain point.[1][4]

If you want, I can also give you a **plan-by-plan pricing comparison** or a **feature checklist for SDR teams**.
